tips.text: Type @ to add a file to the context.
Generate a DEVOXXGENIE.md with /init for project-aware answers.
Try /test, /explain or /review on selected code.
Discover and install MCP servers from the MCP Marketplace in Settings.
Enable Agent Mode to let DevoxxGenie read, search and edit files for you.
Turn on RAG in Settings to add semantic project context automatically.
Drag & drop images into the prompt when using a multimodal model.
Press Ctrl+Space to autocomplete a slash command.
Add the full project to context with large-context models like Gemini.
Run fully local with Ollama, LMStudio, GPT4All or Llama.cpp — no API key needed.
Create your own slash commands in Settings → Commands.
Type /find to search your indexed project semantically with RAG.
Type /search to augment your prompt with a live web search.
Enable web search (Google or Tavily) to ground answers in live results.
Watch the live token count and cost estimate as you build a prompt.
Tune chat memory size in Settings to control how much history the LLM sees.
Set your own submit and newline shortcuts in Settings.
Zoom the chat font with Cmd/Ctrl +/- — the size sticks across restarts.
Enable Inline Completion for AI code suggestions as you type.
Enable Security Scanning — Gitleaks, OpenGrep and Trivy run as agent tools.
Define tasks in Backlog.md and let the Agent implement them from the Spec Browser.
Run prompts through Claude Code, Gemini CLI or Copilot with CLI/ACP Runners.
Drop a SKILL.md file into your skills folder to extend the agent in Agent mode.
Automate agents on events — Code Review on commit, Debug on test failure.
Your conversations are saved — reopen or remove past chats from history.
Exclude Javadoc or extra folders when building Full Project Context in Settings.
In Agent Mode, parallel sub-agents can explore several areas at once.
Customize the system prompt in Settings → System Prompt.
DevoxxGenie is open source — star it on GitHub and join us at Devoxx!
